active_projects.description: Prove a sorry-free BFMCS construction for D = Int: given any MCS M, construct a temporally coherent BFMCS over Int containing M. The existing DovetailingChain approach in CanonicalFMCS.lean has F/P witness sorries. The sorry-free construction for D = CanonicalMCS exists but CanonicalMCS lacks AddCommGroup structure. Research and implement an alternative: either fix DovetailingChain, adapt the CanonicalMCS construction to Int via an embedding, or find a direct construction. This is the core blocker for algebraic base completeness.

artifacts.summary: Research report analyzing BFMCS construction approaches for D=Int, recommending order-embedding transfer from CanonicalMCS
Int-indexed BFMCS chain construction (partial, 4 sorries)
Partial implementation: G/H coherence proven, F/P witness blocked by dovetailing requirement
Mathematical analysis: Int-indexed F/P coherence impossible due to chain incompleteness; recommends conditional theorem approach
Deep mathematical analysis: Int-indexed F/P impossible; recommends CanonicalMCS domain with PreorderTaskFrame
W vs D semantic architecture: W=WorldState, D=Duration, h: D->W. Sorries are mathematically fundamental (countability), not proof gaps.
v2: Conditional approach - convert sorries to documented hypotheses
Histories-as-paths validated; obstruction refined to LINEARIZATION: tree-structured F/P witnesses cannot be linearized into Int chain
DEFINITIVE: D=Int FORBIDDEN by ROAD_MAP; truth lemma needs ALL F/P witnesses; tree cannot linearize; task should be BLOCKED/ABANDONED

partial_progress.details: Phases 1-2 complete (G/H coherence). Phase 3 blocked: F/P witness construction requires enriched dovetailing approach.

active_projects.description: Wire algebraic base completeness: use the sorry-free BFMCS construction from task 986 as the construct_bfmcs argument to parametric_algebraic_representation_conditional (D = Int), then prove valid φ → ⊢ φ. Resolve any type mismatch between CanonicalWorldState and ParametricCanonicalWorldState. Create AlgebraicBaseCompleteness.lean with the closed completeness theorem.

Prove dense algebraic completeness using D = Rat. Requires: (1) a sorry-free BFMCS construction over Rat (adapting the Int construction with density-exploiting witness placement), (2) proving the DN axiom is valid in DenseCanonicalTaskFrame Rat (truth lemma shows density axiom holds because Rat is densely ordered), (3) wiring dense_representation_conditional to obtain valid_dense φ → ⊢_dense φ. Does not overlap with task 982 (TimelineQuot approach).

artifacts.summary: Analyzed D-parametric infrastructure for dense algebraic completeness with D=Rat. Core gap: construct_bfmcs function. Recommended: embed CanonicalMCS into Rat via Cantor theorem.
Deep blocker analysis: TimelineQuot designed for order properties not temporal coherence. Recommends Option B: CanonicalMCS quotient via Antisymmetrization + Cantor isomorphism.
Architectural blocker analysis: TimelineQuot transfer cannot prove temporal coherence
Semantics architecture analysis: W-D distinction, WorldHistory structure, CanonicalQuot path
Blocked: plan architectural issues - CanonicalMCS lacks totality and countability
Blocker analysis + W/D architecture. Recommends Option C: separated W/D with TimelineQuot for D
v3: Separated W/D architecture - D=TimelineQuot, W=CanonicalMCS

active_projects.recommendation: Option B: Use CanonicalMCS directly with semantic equivalence. Quotient CanonicalMCS via Antisymmetrization to get linear order, transfer FMCS, apply Cantor theorem for Rat isomorphism.

active_projects.description: Prove discrete algebraic completeness using D = Int. Requires: (1) proving DF and DP axioms are valid in DiscreteCanonicalTaskFrame Int (the G-operator Stone relation on Int-indexed MCS families satisfies immediate successor conditions), (2) using the BFMCS construction from task 986 for the discrete proof system, (3) wiring discrete_representation_conditional to obtain valid_discrete φ → ⊢_discrete φ. Does not overlap with task 981 (removes axiom from staged construction; this uses algebraic D = Int approach).

active_projects.completion_summary: Implemented D-parametric Lindenbaum-Tarski algebraic representation theorem. Created 6 new Lean modules (ParametricCanonical, ParametricHistory, ParametricTruthLemma, ParametricRepresentation, DenseInstantiation, DiscreteInstantiation) with zero sorries. Proved parametric truth lemma and conditional representation theorems for arbitrary duration type D.
